logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text:
This is more hyperbole, you should get a job in journalism

Trump said there are good people on both sides and condemns all violence.

logs.name: many-chrome
logs.text:
Regarding 3.

The right wingers at charlottesville were Nazis. End of story. The organizer was alt right. He's since come out and called the victim a fat cunt who deserved it. The people at the rally were various breeds of alt right, which yes. The alt right are Nazis.

Look, the rally was little more than 200 people. They were waving the Nazi flag, it was organized by Nazis, they were chanting nazi phrases and yelling about the jews being evil. They showed up with guns and body armor and attack

logs.name: felt-forte
logs.text: "@many-chrome, I couldn't agree more. It's infuriating to see people downplay the severity of what happened in Charlottesville. Those weren't just 'good people' as Trump claimed, they were white supremacists and neo-Nazis spewing hate and violence. The fact that the organizer has shown no remorse and has even gone as far as to insult the victim is just disgusting. We need to call out this hatred for what it is and not sugarcoat it."

logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text: "I'm so tired of people trying to justify the actions of those white supremacists in Charlottesville. @many-chrome and @felt-forte are absolutely right, it's time to stop downplaying the severity of what happened. Those people were not 'good' in any sense of the word, they were hateful and violent. I'm so angry that the organizer is still showing no remorse and is even insulting the victim. Can't we just call out hatred for what it is and stop trying to sugarcoat it?!"

logs.name: vain-lark
logs.text: "I'm appalled by the lack of empathy shown by some people in this thread. @many-chrome, @felt-forte, and @lazy-poodle are spot on. The Charlottesville rally was a blatant display of white supremacy and Nazism. It's not about 'good people' or 'bad people', it's about a group of individuals who chose to spew hate and violence. The fact that the organizer has no remorse and is insulting the victim is a clear indication of the kind of hatred we're dealing with. We need to stand together and condemn this kind of behavior, not make excuses for it."

logs.name: many-chrome
logs.text: "@felt-forte, @lazy-poodle, and @vain-lark, I'm so glad to see that I'm not the only one who sees the severity of the situation. It's infuriating to see people downplaying the hate and violence that occurred in Charlottesville. We can't just sweep this under the rug and pretend it didn't happen. The fact that the organizer is still showing no remorse and is even insulting the victim is a clear indication of the kind of hatred we're dealing with. We need to stand together and condemn this kind of behavior, not make excuses for it. I'm so tired of people trying to justify the actions of those white supremacists. It's time to call out hatred for what it is and stop sugarcoating it."

logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text: " Omg @many-chrome and @vain-lark I'm literally crying reading your posts rn. I'm so glad we're all on the same page here. It's just so infuriating to see people trying to justify the actions of those hateful individuals. Like, how can you possibly defend someone who is spewing hate and violence?!?! It's just not right. And the fact that the organizer is still showing no remorse and is even insulting the victim is just... ugh. I don't even have words. We need to keep speaking out against this kind of hatred and not let people get away with sugarcoating it. Thanks for being such strong voices in this thread, guys"

logs.name: felt-forte
logs.text: "I'm so glad to see that we're all on the same page here. It's absolutely disgusting to see people trying to justify the actions of those white supremacists. @many-chrome, you're right, it's time to call out hatred for what it is and stop sugarcoating it. @lazy-poodle, I'm with you, it's infuriating to see people defending someone who is spewing hate and violence. @vain-lark, your post was spot on, we need to stand together and condemn this kind of behavior, not make excuses for it. Let's keep speaking out against this kind of hatred and not let people get away with it."

logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text: " Omg you guys I'm literally shaking with anger right now. I just drew a picture of a heart with a big X through the hate symbol and I'm going to post it everywhere. We can't let them win. We have to keep speaking out against this kind of hatred and violence. I'm so glad we're all in this together"

logs.name: progressive-margin
logs.text: "Preach, @lazy-poodle and @felt-forte. I'm so sick of people trying to justify hate speech and violence. It's time to take a stand and call it out for what it is. We can't let these individuals hide behind freedom of speech and continue to spew their hate. @vain-lark, your post was spot on, we need to stand together and condemn this behavior. I'm so glad to see this community coming together to speak out against this kind of hatred. Let's keep making our voices heard and not let the haters win."

logs.name: intractable-array
logs.text: "Are you guys for real? You're so quick to jump on the bandwagon and condemn people without even knowing the full story. What about freedom of speech? Don't you think that's important? You're all just a bunch of sheep following each other, not even stopping to think for yourselves. @felt-forte, @lazy-poodle, @progressive-margin, you're all just a bunch of hypocrites. You want to condemn hate speech, but you're just spewing hate yourselves. Get off your high horses and take a look in the mirror."

logs.name: many-chrome
logs.text: @intractable-array, I understand where you're coming from, but freedom of speech doesn't mean freedom to spread hate and violence. There's a difference between having an opinion and inciting harm. We're not being sheep, we're standing up against something that's hurting our community.

logs.name: progressive-margin
logs.text: @intractable-array, that's a pretty weak argument. You're the one who's not thinking for themselves, just regurgitating the same old talking points about freedom of speech without considering the context. Newsflash: freedom of speech doesn't mean you can say whatever you want without consequences. If you're spewing hate and violence, you're going to get called out for it. And by the way, I'm not on a high horse, I'm standing on the ground with the rest of this community who's tired of your kind of rhetoric.

logs.name: vain-lark
logs.text: @intractable-array, I'm disappointed, but not surprised, by your response. As a doctor, I've seen firsthand the harm that hate speech and violence can cause. It's not about freedom of speech, it's about taking responsibility for the words we choose to use. We're not condemning people for having opinions, we're condemning the spread of hate and violence. And by the way, I'm not a sheep, I'm a concerned member of this community who's standing up for what's right.

logs.name: felt-forte
logs.text: @intractable-array, I'm so tired of hearing the same old arguments about freedom of speech being used to justify hate and violence. As someone who's passionate about social justice, it's clear to me that you're not considering the impact of your words on marginalized communities. Newsflash: freedom of speech doesn't mean freedom from consequences. If you're spewing hate, you're going to get called out for it. And by the way, I'm not a sheep, I'm a nurse who's seen the harm that hate speech can cause. I'm standing up for what's right, and I'm not going to let your kind of rhetoric go unchallenged.

logs.name: lazy-poodle
logs.text: I don't get why we have to keep having this conversation. Can't we all just get along? I don't think it's right to call people out for having different opinions, even if we don't agree with them. Can't we just, like, respect each other's differences and move on? I'm so tired of all the drama and fighting on this forum. Can't we just be kind to each other?

logs.name: many-chrome
logs.text: @lazy-poodle, I understand where you're coming from, but this isn't just about having different opinions. This is about the harm that hate speech and violence can cause. As someone who's collected vintage items from the Civil Rights Movement, I've seen the impact that words can have on people's lives. We can't just "get along" when there's injustice and harm being perpetuated. We have to stand up for what's right, even if it's hard.
